title: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.20 — Cisco ISE Network Segregation Matrix Drift Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.20 requires segregation of networks. UC-17.1.76 produces continuous machine-readable evidence of segmentation-policy stability via daily diff against the approved baseline.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrate that the ISE TrustSec / Group-Based Policy matrix (the heart of microsegmentation policy) remains aligned to the approved baseline; any drift triggers compliance review.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.58' running on `ise_sgt_matrix.csv` lookup vs. baseline; archived to `audit_evidence` with `iso27001_clause=A.8.20`.

dataSources: `ise_sgt_matrix.csv` (current export from ISE OpenAPI) and `ise_sgt_matrix_baseline.csv` (approved baseline). Cross-reference UC-17.1.76.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263) + Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915).
spl:
| inputlookup ise_sgt_matrix.csv
| join type=outer source_sgt destination_sgt [| inputlookup ise_sgt_matrix_baseline.csv | rename action as baseline_action]
| eval drift=if(action!=baseline_action, "DRIFTED", "ok")
| where drift=="DRIFTED"
| eval iso27001_clause="A.8.20", control="Network_Segregation"
| collect index=audit_evidence
description: We continuously evidence ISO 27001 A.8.20 (network segregation) by detecting any drift in the ISE TrustSec / GBP segmentation matrix.
value: Segmentation drift is an insidious erosion of A.8.20. Daily baseline comparison turns operational change-detection into audit-grade evidence with attribution.
implementation: Daily schedule. Baseline must be checked into version control with an approved-by attribution.
visualization: A.8.20 panel: drift cell timeline; current vs. baseline matrix.

references.title: UC-17.1.76 — ISE Group-Based Policy (GBP) Effective-Policy Drift

knownFalsePositives: 1. Baseline pending update during planned segmentation change — coordinate with policy engineering and update baseline post-CAB.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.76 deployed. Lookups present.
- Baseline `ise_sgt_matrix_baseline.csv` checked into Git with approved-by metadata.

## Step 1 — Data

Daily OpenAPI export script populates `ise_sgt_matrix.csv`.

## Step 2 — Implementation

```ini
[UC-22.6.58: ISO 27001 A.8.20 — ISE Segmentation Matrix Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 7 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1m
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.6.58,reg=ISO27001,clause=A.8.20
```

## Step 3 — Validate

Deliberately introduce a drift cell, confirm UC-22.6.58 captures it within 24h.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

A.8.20 dashboard with drift count panel and current vs. baseline matrix viewer.

## Step 5 — Audit submission

Monthly evidence pack with attestation that baseline was updated only via the change board.
grandmaExplanation: We continuously evidence ISO 27001 A.8.20 (network segregation) by checking the segmentation matrix against the approved baseline daily. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Modify a single SGT-matrix cell. UC-22.6.58 logs drift evidence next day.
controlTest.negativeScenario: No drift. Empty evidence set; A.8.20 attestation held.

evidence: Saved search results archived to `audit_evidence` with ISO 27001 A.8.20 tagging.
exclusions: Lab and test SGT matrices excluded.
